metadata.title: Test Suite Tier Redesign: Staging / Prod / Remote
metadata.rationale:
The current test tiers conflate "code tests" with "infrastructure tests" with "GPU instance validation." This causes confusing failures and wasted time.
Key decisions from user: - "staging" means Docker Compose services running exactly as they would in prod - "dev" (bare metal, no Docker) is intentionally unsupported for now - SAM3 model loading is BANNED from CI — it belongs in gpu_instance/ only - Docker Compose services MUST be running for prod tests - Need separate "remote" tier for cloud/intranet infrastructure validation

plan.user-context: User's vision (verbatim): - "staging environment means I have the Dockers running as they would be on prod" - "dev would be left for future use — scenarios when someone wants to run with scissors" - "test-prod that enforces stack is up" - "test-prod-remote that could work with intranet on-prem deployment, and Pulumi-based cloud stack on GCP" - SAM3 model loading tests are NEVER in any CI tier — they validate new instance types only

current-state.tier: Code-only tests. No models, no services. Fast PR gate for main. 5404 passed, 2 skipped. Clean.

Includes Docker Compose service tests that fail without stack running. Also included SAM3 model loading tests (now MOVED to gpu_instance/). After SAM3 removal: 5989 passed, 3 failed (Docker training smoke), 161 skipped.
tests/gpu_instance/ only. SAM3 forward passes, large model training. Run on RunPod RTX 4090 or GCP L4. Never in standard CI.

proposed-tiers.description: 5-tier architecture that cleanly separates by infrastructure requirements. Each tier builds on the previous: staging ⊂ prod ⊂ prod-remote.

tier.what: Pure unit tests + config validation. No models, no services, no data.
tier.marker-exclusions: not model_loading, not slow, not integration, not requires_services
tier.when-to-run: Every PR targeting main. Must pass before merge.

tier.what: All code tests INCLUDING service integration tests. Docker Compose stack MUST be running (MLflow, MinIO, Prefect, PostgreSQL). This validates the full local environment matches production behavior.
tier.pre-check: Before running tests, verify services are healthy: - MLflow at localhost:5000 - MinIO at localhost:9000 - Prefect at localhost:4200 - PostgreSQL at localhost:5432 If ANY service is unreachable, FAIL FAST with instructions (not silently skip).
tier.when-to-run: Before promoting main → prod. After major changes.
tier.includes: - All staging tests - Docker Compose service health tests (MLflow, MinIO, Prefect) - Inter-service contract tests (MLflow-MinIO artifact round-trip) - Docker training smoke tests (debug run in container) - Dashboard smoke tests (API + UI) - E2E pipeline tests (full flow chain) - Biostatistics flow integration tests - ONNX export tests (DynUNet/MambaVesselNet, NOT SAM3)
tier.excludes: - SAM3 model loading (BANNED from CI — tests/gpu_instance/ only) - Cloud/remote infrastructure tests (requires_remote marker) - tests/v2/quasi_e2e/ (parametrized model×loss, too slow)

tier.what: Tests that validate cloud or intranet on-prem infrastructure. Requires either GCP Pulumi stack or intranet deployment.
tier.prerequisite: For GCP: gcloud auth, Pulumi stack healthy, GCS buckets accessible For intranet: VPN connected, on-prem MLflow reachable
tier.when-to-run: - After Pulumi infrastructure changes - After cloud provider configuration changes - Validating new intranet deployment - NOT part of routine PR/promotion workflow
tier.includes: - Cloud MLflow connectivity (tests/v2/cloud/) - SkyPilot GCP access verification - DVC GCS remote accessibility - GAR Docker image availability - Pulumi stack health queries - Remote MLflow artifact round-trip

tier.what: Tests that validate MLflow artifacts, checkpoints, metric integrity after a training run completes. Validates the MLflow-as-contract guarantee.
tier.prerequisite: mlruns/ contains completed training runs with slash-prefix metrics
tier.when-to-run: - After debug factorial run completes - After production factorial run completes - Validating that all MLflow artifacts are correct before biostatistics
tier.includes: - mlruns integrity tests (production runs identified, metrics present) - Checkpoint loadability (all checkpoints exist and load) - DuckDB extraction from real mlruns - Pyfunc registration from real checkpoints - Per-volume metric verification

tier.what: SAM3 model loading, forward passes, ONNX export, BentoML serving. Run once per new GPU instance type (L4, A100, RTX 4090). NEVER in any CI tier. NEVER on dev machine (8 GB VRAM).
tier.prerequisite: GPU with >=16 GB VRAM, SAM3 weights downloaded
tier.when-to-run: - Validating new GPU instance type works with SAM3 - After SAM3 adapter code changes - After ONNX export changes - On RunPod RTX 4090 or GCP L4 only
tier.includes: - tests/gpu_instance/ ONLY - SAM3 Vanilla/TopoLoRA/Hybrid forward + backward passes - SAM3 ONNX export + onnxruntime validation - MONAI Deploy SAM3 operator tests - BentoML SAM3 import tests - MLflow SAM3 pyfunc serving tests

phase.name: Marker Registration
phase.task: Register new markers in pyproject.toml: requires_services, requires_remote, requires_mlruns
Add @pytest.mark.requires_services to all Docker Compose service tests
Add @pytest.mark.requires_mlruns to all real mlruns data tests

phase.name: Makefile Targets
phase.task: Update make test-prod: add service health pre-check (fail fast if stack not up)
Add make test-prod-remote target with cloud/intranet prerequisites
Add make test-post-training target for mlruns validation
Update make test-staging to explicitly exclude requires_services

phase.name: Documentation
phase.task: Update CLAUDE.md Test Tiers table with 5 tiers
Remove temporary skip guards once proper tier separation is in place

phase.name: SAM3 Cleanup (DONE)
phase.task: Move SAM3 tests from tests/v2/integration/ → tests/gpu_instance/
Add metalearning doc for SAM3-in-CI ban
Remove VRAM guard code from moved tests (not needed in gpu_instance/)
